rows.item_text: Did the ventilator tube partially detach at the mask end, with no alarm sounding from the machine?
Did the contractor switch the alarm back on before leaving the hospital?
Was the patient stabilised with manual ventilation, and was a formal inquest convened?
Did the patient experience a heart attack?
Did the contractor disable the ventilator alarm to run diagnostics?
Was a new overnight staffing policy announced, and was the nurse's patient list longer than usual during her night shift?

rows.prompt_text: If the hospital administrator had not announced reduced overnight staffing, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?
If the contractor had switched the alarm back on before leaving, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?
If the hospital administrator had not announced reduced overnight staffing, would the contractor still have disabled the ventilator alarm during maintenance?
If the hospital had not convened a formal inquest, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?

If the nurse had not been assigned a longer patient list than usual, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?
If an alarm had sounded when the ventilator stopped delivering air, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?
If the ventilator tube had not partially detached from the patient's mask, would the patient still have ended up in respiratory distress?

rows.item_text: Did the council approve the venue permit in early June, and was a first-aid team assigned by the volunteer coordination office for the day of the fair?
Was the temperature by midday well above what had been forecast, and did the organiser open the rest area earlier than planned?
Was the first-aid team called away to another incident, and did their replacement take nearly two hours to arrive?
Did the council convene a review of the venue booking, volunteer scheduling, and weather conditions?
Was the fair cancelled due to rain?
Was a first-aid team present on site when the organiser called an ambulance for the elderly man?

rows.prompt_text: If a first-aid team had not been assigned to the event, would the temperature by midday still have been higher than forecast?
If the temperature by midday had not been higher than forecast, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If a first-aid team had not been assigned to the event, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If the first-aid team had not been called away to another incident, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If the organiser had not opened the rest area and handed out water, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If a replacement first-aid team had arrived within a few minutes, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If the organiser had not booked an outdoor venue for the fair, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?
If the council had not convened a review of the event, would the elderly man still have become unresponsive in the sun?

rows.item_text: Did the teacher decide to turn the group back at the high point?
Did one of the younger students show early signs of hypothermia under the trees, and did a review board examine the teacher's decisions the following week?
Did a student injure her ankle after losing footing on a wet rock?
Did a member of the group suffer a concussion from the fall?
Was there no phone signal in the valley, and did the teacher send two older students back toward the car park to call for rescue?
Did the teacher choose a mountain trail that looped through a wooded section, and did she note that several students were wearing light jackets rather than waterproofs?

rows.prompt_text: If a review board had not examined the teacher's decisions, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If the student had not lost footing on a wet rock and injured her ankle, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If heavy rain had not arrived on the ridge, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If the group had set out with full wet-weather gear for every student, would heavy rain still have arrived on the ridge?
If the teacher had not split the group to send two students for help, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If the group had kept a phone signal in the valley, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If the group had set out with full wet-weather gear for every student, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?
If the teacher had not chosen a mountain trail for the trip, would a student still have shown signs of hypothermia under the trees?

rows.item_text: Did the restaurant's sprinkler system activate during the fire?
Did the technician replace the old ducts behind the walls?
Did smoke spread into the dining room through the ceiling, and did a fire investigation examine the technician's work order?
Did a health inspector tell the owner the ventilation system was not up to standard, and did the owner call the first available technician rather than the original installer?
Was the kitchen fully booked with all six burners running when a low whump came from inside the wall after a line cook lit the back burner?
Did the sous chef notice a faint smell near the back burner within hours of the technician's visit?

rows.prompt_text: If the kitchen had not been fully booked with all burners running, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the wall had not ignited when a cook lit the back burner, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the owner had called the original installer rather than the first available technician, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the technician had replaced the old ducts along with the fan, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the sous chef had not noticed a faint smell near the back burner, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If a fire investigation had not examined the sequence of events, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the health inspector had not flagged the ventilation as substandard, would smoke still have spread into the dining room and the restaurant been evacuated?
If the technician had replaced the old ducts along with the fan, would the kitchen still have been fully booked with all burners running?
